title: Architect WorldCup LLM research dossier
description: Frozen, committed per-match research dossier for one fixture. Phase 1 (web search enabled) writes it; Phase 2 (no tools) reads only this. Its commit, before the fixture's kickoff, is the leakage proof, and it is the reproducibility anchor for Predictions B and C, which are not bit-reproducible. The coverage manifest records, per factor cell, how many raw search hits came back and how many survived the quarantine and tier filter, so a cell that was researched and quarantined is never mistaken for a clean neutral.

round.description: Target round this dossier informs. GROUP for a group-stage fixture, otherwise the knockout round.

match.description: Fixture identifier for the tie this dossier covers.

home_team.description: Nominal home: the first-listed team in the official fixture. A labelling convention only, since the venue is neutral for knockout ties.

as_of_date.description: Cutoff: the dossier holds nothing dated after this, the day before the fixture kicks off.

committed_at.description: Git commit time of the dossier, the before-kickoff leakage proof. Null until committed.

git_sha.description: Git commit sha of the code that produced the dossier.

model_version.description: Version tag of the LLM analyst layer.

provenance.description: Research-call provenance: model, effort, web search tool version, allow-list, max_uses, retrieved_at, usage, cost.

factors.description: Admissible findings organised into the fixed factor taxonomy, each entry carrying its source tier. A factor array holds only findings that survived the quarantine gate and the tier filter; the coverage manifest records what was filtered out.

coverage.description: One cell per factor, the research coverage manifest. Raw hits are recorded separately from admissible findings so a cell that returned content the quarantine gate or tier filter then removed shows n_raw_hits greater than zero with n_admissible_findings zero, and is never silently treated as a clean neutral. Prediction time may mark a factor insufficient_evidence only when its cell status is queried_no_findings or unresearchable.

n_raw_hits.description: Count of raw search results that addressed this factor, before the quarantine gate and tier filter.

n_admissible_findings.description: Count of findings that survived the quarantine gate and tier filter, equal to the length of this factor's array in factors.

status.description: has_findings: admissible findings exist. queried_no_findings: the cell was searched and nothing came back. quarantined_or_filtered: raw content came back but the quarantine gate or tier filter removed all of it, so this is NOT a clean neutral. unresearchable: the cell could not be researched (no admissible source covered it), a stated blind spot that prediction time must surface in the rationale.

researchable.description: False only for an unresearchable cell, where no admissible source covered the factor for this fixture.

note.description: Optional detail: why the cell is unresearchable, or what raw content was quarantined or tier-filtered out.

factorEntries.description: Admissible findings for one factor, those that passed the quarantine gate and the tier filter. May be empty, in which case the coverage manifest cell distinguishes queried_no_findings, quarantined_or_filtered, and unresearchable. Prediction time may mark this factor insufficient_evidence only for the first and last of those.

claim.description: The retrieved fact, in plain language.

source.description: Where the fact came from: an allow-listed outlet domain or URL. No betting or prediction-market sources, no open-edit or anonymous sources, by design.

source_tier.description: Source reputability tier: 1 official federations, confederations, and top international wires; 2 reputable national sports press; 3 weaker but still named and reputable. Anonymous, open-edit, forum, and betting sources are excluded entirely, not tiered.

team.description: Team the fact concerns, if specific to one side.

meeting_date.description: For a historical head-to-head fact, the date the two teams met. The quarantine gate preserves a head-to-head between the fixture teams only if this is strictly before the cutoff; a missing or on-or-after-cutoff date is a leak and fails the gate.

retrieved_at.description: When the fact was retrieved, for provenance.
